capabilities: Ensure payment data handling complies with PCI DSS by tokenizing card data and minimizing the cardholder data environment scope.

procedure.action: Assert input format by checking that all payment data fields conform to PCI DSS requirements using a JSON Schema validator; reject any non-compliant input with an error code by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Tokenize card data by replacing card numbers with tokens using a PCI-compliant tokenization service; ensure tokens are stored securely and separately from other cardholder data using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Scope cardholder environment by isolating systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data using network segmentation; apply firewall rules to restrict access to these systems using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Log data access by configuring audit logs to capture all access attempts to cardholder data; ensure logs are immutable and stored in a secure, centralized logging system using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Finalize compliance by conducting a PCI DSS self-assessment questionnaire (SAQ) annually; document all procedures and maintain evidence of compliance for audit purposes using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Procedure for PCI-compliant payment data handling: tokenize card data, scope environment, log access.
title: PCI-Compliant Payment Data Handling

failureConditions: Data breach when card data is not tokenized, causing exposure of sensitive information to unauthorized parties.
Non-compliance when cardholder environment is not properly scoped, leading to increased audit scope and potential fines.
Unauthorized access when data access is not logged, causing inability to detect and respond to security incidents.
metrics: input format by checking that all payment data fields conform to PCI DSS requirements using a JSON Schema validator
